name: Safety Risk Framing appraisal for Tad Cardiovascular Endothelial Bp bundle
summary: Accepted. The baseline keeps safety near every vascular interpretation: it reuses label, nitrate, alpha-blocker, and antihypertensive safety findings; notes tadalafil adverse-event signals in Pattanaik, MAKROTAD, PHIRST, and ETLAS-2; and frames blood-pressure lowering as context-dependent rather than inherently beneficial.

findings.claim_or_issue: The claim includes nitrate/riociguat contraindications and cautions for alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, alcohol, cardiovascular disease, renal/hepatic impairment, and CYP3A4 inhibitors.
findings.why_it_matters: Any tadalafil vascular effect can become a safety issue in interacting or vulnerable contexts.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged findings include more adverse reactions in Pattanaik 2019, more common PDE5 adverse effects in MAKROTAD, common PAH adverse events in PHIRST, and 76% versus 36% adverse events in ETLAS-2.
findings.why_it_matters: Benefit-oriented cardiovascular summaries must not suppress tolerability boundaries.

findings.claim_or_issue: Full adverse-event tables were not extracted for every trial in this cardiovascular baseline.
findings.why_it_matters: The baseline is safe for qualitative framing, but not for comparative tolerability rates across cardiovascular subpopulations.
findings.recommended_action: Use the dedicated safety baseline for public safety caveats and retrieve full trial reports before comparative AE synthesis.
